source_refs.section: public-safe topic: DDD starts by locating valuable business problems and model boundaries before ch

summary: DDD starts by locating valuable business problems and model boundaries before choosing implementation patterns.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: DDD tactical patterns are selected after the business boundary and model complex

summary: DDD tactical patterns are selected after the business boundary and model complexity are understood.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: Strategic design begins with the business domain rather than technology structur

summary: Strategic design begins with the business domain rather than technology structure.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: Core subdomains are the differentiating subset of a business domain and deserve

summary: Core subdomains are the differentiating subset of a business domain and deserve disproportionate modeling care.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: Supporting subdomains matter to operations but usually do not justify the same i

summary: Supporting subdomains matter to operations but usually do not justify the same investment as core differentiators.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: Generic subdomains are common capabilities where reuse, purchase, or commodity i

summary: Generic subdomains are common capabilities where reuse, purchase, or commodity implementation is often the rational choice.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: Core domains are the strongest candidates for richer domain models because the b

summary: Core domains are the strongest candidates for richer domain models because the business payoff can justify the extra design work.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: Commodity logic often benefits more from simple procedural implementation than f

summary: Commodity logic often benefits more from simple procedural implementation than from a rich model.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: Supporting CRUD-heavy domains may fit data-centric patterns when invariants are

summary: Supporting CRUD-heavy domains may fit data-centric patterns when invariants are modest.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: Domain experts supply the examples and distinctions needed to keep the shared la

summary: Domain experts supply the examples and distinctions needed to keep the shared language meaningful.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: Raw business language becomes more useful when refined into a bounded, model-bac

summary: Raw business language becomes more useful when refined into a bounded, model-backed ubiquitous language.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: A ubiquitous language is reliable only inside the boundary where its terms carry

summary: A ubiquitous language is reliable only inside the boundary where its terms carry the same meaning.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: Context maps make bounded contexts and their relationships visible for design an

summary: Context maps make bounded contexts and their relationships visible for design and planning decisions.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: Partnership is one context-map relationship when teams can coordinate closely.

summary: Partnership is one context-map relationship when teams can coordinate closely.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: Shared kernel is a context-map relationship that shares a small model subset und

summary: Shared kernel is a context-map relationship that shares a small model subset under strict coordination.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: Conformist relationships describe downstream acceptance of upstream model decisi

summary: Conformist relationships describe downstream acceptance of upstream model decisions.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: An anticorruption layer appears on a context map where a local model needs prote

summary: An anticorruption layer appears on a context map where a local model needs protection from an external model.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: Open host service is a context-map integration pattern where an upstream provide

summary: Open host service is a context-map integration pattern where an upstream provides a stable public interface.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: Shared kernels weaken context independence unless the shared part stays intentio

summary: Shared kernels weaken context independence unless the shared part stays intentionally small and governed.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: An anticorruption layer protects bounded context language and invariants from ex

summary: An anticorruption layer protects bounded context language and invariants from external model drift.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: Simple procedural logic can move toward active record when data structures and p

summary: Simple procedural logic can move toward active record when data structures and persistence behavior need a modest home.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: Active record becomes insufficient when behavior, invariants, and lifecycle rule

summary: Active record becomes insufficient when behavior, invariants, and lifecycle rules need a richer model.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: Entities model identity and lifecycle within a rich domain model.

summary: Entities model identity and lifecycle within a rich domain model.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: Value objects make descriptive business values explicit without adding identity

summary: Value objects make descriptive business values explicit without adding identity semantics.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: Aggregates organize model objects around consistency boundaries and invariant en

summary: Aggregates organize model objects around consistency boundaries and invariant enforcement.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: Repositories separate aggregate retrieval and persistence from domain behavior.

summary: Repositories separate aggregate retrieval and persistence from domain behavior.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: Domain events expose meaningful facts that occurred inside the model without for

summary: Domain events expose meaningful facts that occurred inside the model without forcing direct coupling.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: Repositories usually operate at aggregate boundaries rather than exposing arbitr

summary: Repositories usually operate at aggregate boundaries rather than exposing arbitrary persistence internals.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: Aggregates can publish domain events to communicate meaningful state changes out

summary: Aggregates can publish domain events to communicate meaningful state changes outside their immediate transaction boundary.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: Domain events become safer integration signals when their meaning is grounded in

summary: Domain events become safer integration signals when their meaning is grounded in a bounded model.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: Event sourcing relies on an append-only event store to retain event history as t

summary: Event sourcing relies on an append-only event store to retain event history as the source of state.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: Event-sourced models commonly rebuild aggregate state from historical events.

summary: Event-sourced models commonly rebuild aggregate state from historical events.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: CQRS often complements event sourcing because command history and query models h

summary: CQRS often complements event sourcing because command history and query models have different optimization needs.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: CQRS may use event-store data to build read models without forcing command stora

summary: CQRS may use event-store data to build read models without forcing command storage and query storage into one shape.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: Event sourcing is usually overkill for simple procedural domains where history i

summary: Event sourcing is usually overkill for simple procedural domains where history is not central to the business model.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: CQRS should not be introduced merely for structure when a simple write/read path

summary: CQRS should not be introduced merely for structure when a simple write/read path is sufficient.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: Event storming uses meaningful events to discover process flow, decisions, and h

summary: Event storming uses meaningful events to discover process flow, decisions, and hidden domain vocabulary.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: Event storming can surface candidate aggregates after commands, policies, and in

summary: Event storming can surface candidate aggregates after commands, policies, and invariants become visible.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: Event storming helps test whether candidate context boundaries match actual busi

summary: Event storming helps test whether candidate context boundaries match actual business flows and language shifts.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: Bounded contexts are a strong input for microservice boundaries, but deployment

summary: Bounded contexts are a strong input for microservice boundaries, but deployment boundaries still require operational judgment.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: Treating every bounded context as a service can create unnecessary distributed-s

summary: Treating every bounded context as a service can create unnecessary distributed-system cost.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: DDD boundaries clarify who owns events and what each event means across integrat

summary: DDD boundaries clarify who owns events and what each event means across integration boundaries.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: Event-driven integrations may still need translation boundaries when event meani

summary: Event-driven integrations may still need translation boundaries when event meaning differs between contexts.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: Domain boundaries can guide data product ownership in a data mesh architecture.

summary: Domain boundaries can guide data product ownership in a data mesh architecture.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: Useful domain-owned data products need language that analysts and operational te

summary: Useful domain-owned data products need language that analysts and operational teams interpret consistently.

source_refs.section: public-safe topic: Microservice decomposition should follow strategic understanding, not technology

summary: Microservice decomposition should follow strategic understanding, not technology fashion or premature service slicing.
